edict: Spread truth, debate contentious issues, aid messengers
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Eritrice](/Deities.aspx?ID=470)
(Heart-Speaker)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 308

The Heart-Speaker Eritrice represents honest debate, opinions, and truth. Facts and information are important to Eritrice, but truths and wisdom gained through discussion are far more valuable to her than those gained through books. She maintains there is nothing wrong with disagreeing so long as the disagreement is respectful, and that it is crucial to be open to discussion, willing to consider new information, and receptive to forming new opinions. Opinions are valuable in that they help understand other views, but Eritrice reminds her followers that opinions are not facts and can be incorrect and even harmful. When lies are spoken or become the rule of the land, those that follow Eritrice work through networks of like-minded individuals to spread the truth using messages sent to all who will listen. 

In her mortal form, the Heart-Speaker appears as a muscular, 8-foot-tall woman with a female [lion’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2866) head. She wears a rose-colored breastplate over a short kilt. When she speaks, her voice is so entrancing and compelling that those she appears to often don’t take note of any of her other features. Those who value truth, from debaters to town criers to lawmakers, are among the followers of Eritrice.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Spread truth, debate contentious issues, aid messengers

**Anathema** Sow or perpetuate lies, obstruct discussion, argue in bad faith

Debate, opinions, truth

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Falayna](/Deities.aspx?ID=471)
(Warrior’s Ring)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 74

Femininity, martial training, and rings are the purview of the Warrior’s Ring. In the eyes of Falayna, there is as much grace and beauty in the martial arts as exists in any culture’s definition of femininity. The strength of womanhood is a hallmark of femininity, and strength in arms reflects this, with the flourish and personality of a fighting style a vibrant means for selfexpression. Followers of Falayna learn to fight so they can both express their body and defend themselves if necessary, and strive to feel beautiful doing so, both in form and in dress. Falayna also enjoys rings, and as such she is associated with events in which rings are given or exchanged, such as weddings.

When the Warrior’s Ring appears to mortals, her form is most often that of a muscular woman with the hair and eye colors most commonly associated with feminine beauty in the mortal’s mind. She wears a bright silver breastplate on top of flowing, silken robes, her longsword Betrothal sheathed across her back. On her fingers she wears rings of varying colors, styles, and stones; this jewelry seems to constantly shift, as if her fingers wear all the rings found in all the worlds. Followers of Falayna include warriors, soldiers, squires, jewelers, and those engaged to be married.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Wear and make beautiful things, train for combat, recover and return lost mementos

**Anathema** Disrupt or destroy romantic unions, enforce a dress code, cower from fights

Femininity, martial training, rings

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Irez](/Deities.aspx?ID=472)
(Lady of Inscribed Wonder)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 308

Cards, glyphs, scribes, and spells are all the purview of the Lady of Inscribed Wonder. She holds knowledge and understanding of the great power and symbolism behind runes. In particular, Irez understands how runes can supplement and empower arcane magic. It’s through this understanding that Irez is able to make enigmatic predictions on events in the distant future, which more often than not come to pass. Many of her followers are calligraphers, gamblers, harrow readers and others who regularly handle cards and symbols, be they for an arcane purpose, prophetic practices, or their more mundane applications.

Those who have seen Irez, either in person or through the cards, describe her as a woman with the body of a powerful serpent and large wings resembling those of a [bat](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=369). Irez is constantly draped with strips of parchment, each displaying glyphs that bear the secret to a particular spell or specific cards, such as the harrow’s Tyrant card.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Read fortunes, practice calligraphy, devise and study runes

**Anathema** Destroy magic scrolls, cheat at games of chance, deliberately write illegibly

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Kelinahat](/Deities.aspx?ID=473)
(She of Ebon Wings)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 308

While most gods prefer to be easily found by those that would worship them, Kelinahat prefers to stay out of the limelight, sticking to the shadows where she is most likely to hear that which others would not. When she wraps her smoky black wings around her slight frame, she is almost impossible to see except in the brightest of sunlight. Even then, she resembles only the vaguest shape of a winged [dwarven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=59) woman made entirely of shadow and difficult to discern, as if one’s eyes refuse to focus on her. She uses this ability to hide in places of torment and torture, working to find information that can be used to free those who have been wrongfully imprisoned. Once she has this, she ensures it makes it to the eyes and ears of those who can use it, keeping herself just as invisible, not wanting others to know where the intelligence came from. 

Those more suited to thrilling heroics sometimes question why She of Ebon Wings and her mortal followers would spend their efforts gathering information rather than taking decisive action when faced with suffering. They see these spies as time wasters at best, and actively detrimental to others at worst. Her followers are judged as pilferers and poachers, always taking what doesn’t belong to them. But those who follow Kelinahat are far more cautious than outsiders would ever consider. They know that even with the information they can provide, due process must be observed, even if it comes to the wrong conclusions. Ignoring this would be to risk that lawful authorities might be forced to take a harsher view of those that they sought to help in the first place.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Gather valuable intelligence, train in the art of espionage, use information you learn to free the innocent

**Anathema** Throw away useful information, destroy evidence of wrongdoing, willingly give information to those of evil intent

**Area of Concern**
Intelligence, spies, stealth

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Keltheald](/Deities.aspx?ID=474)
(The Sunset Spires)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 308

The Sunset Spires stands true, watching as the sun falls to greet the horizon each day. Just as they see the beauty in each sunset, they also see it in every creation that walks, swims, and flies upon [Golarion](/Rules.aspx?ID=3298). Keltheald spends most of their time traveling from one natural wonder to another, enjoying a different view every day. They most often take the form of a kestrel with vibrant wings the colors of a summer sunset, using this form to inconspicuously interact with the locations that they visit.

Followers say that if one finds a kestrel feather reflective of the sky, it’s a sign that one will travel to a new location soon. As a result, those who hope to leave where they are will often scour areas of natural beauty for kestrel feathers, carrying them for luck and safety as they go from place to place. Keltheald encourages this practice, making certain to always leave a feather in any location that they visit.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Take the time to meditate at sunrise or sunset every day, seek out wondrous natural locations, travel to unfamiliar locales, create art reflective of natural beauty

**Anathema** Destroy natural locations and resources, willingly stay indoors for more than a day, never travel outside of your hometown

**Area of Concern**
Natural formations, sunsets, vistas

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Korada](/Deities.aspx?ID=475)
(The Open Hand of Harmony)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 308

Korada, the Open Hand of Harmony, is concerned with foresight, forgiveness, and peace. In particular, he believes that although the tireless fight against wickedness is admirable, the ultimate triumph over evil will come in the form of redemption rather than destruction. Korada’s dedication to peace is such that he and his followers refuse to cause harm to their attackers, instead using their martial skills only to defend themselves. Many Koradans seek greater wisdom through study or meditation in hopes of better understanding their foes so as to guide them toward redemption. This dedication to self-awareness, philosophy, and introspection is said to have allowed Korada greater insight into the workings of [the universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), granting him the great gift of foresight. Korada rarely acts on his visions, however, believing the struggle that comes with true change is always worthwhile, whether or not such a change is successful. 

The Open Hand of Harmony takes the form of a lithe, athletic man donning simple robes. He is covered in sparse, light-brown fur and has three monkey tails that constantly writhe behind him, much like the dancing flames of a powerful fire. Most of Korada’s followers are those who have lived imbalanced lives or faced persistent violence as they seek to find peace in their life and within themselves. Many of the evil individuals redeemed by Koradans soon become followers themselves.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Forgive those who have wronged you, embrace a peaceful mindset, seek and allow redemption

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Lalaci](/Deities.aspx?ID=476)
(He of Motley Repose)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 308

He of Motley Repose champions not only the beauties that are rainbows and relaxation, but also the true importance of self-worth. He teaches that the only way to truly find rest is to know and value oneself above all else. His worshippers are known best for being protectors of the oasis in the desert, for Lalaci knows that having a safe place to rest and relax is just as important as taking the time to be self-reflective and understand yourself. Those who are not protectors use the beauty and versatility of the sands to create works of art, which attempt to mimic the perfect reflection of color found in the beauty of rainbows that come after a storm. 

In his mortal form, Lalaci is a human with light skin and hair, and piercing golden eyes. His prismatic wings form a rainbow cloak, shimmering in the sun for anyone that sees him. When he walks among mortals, he is most often found admiring the wares of artisans, inspiring them to find new ways to bring rainbows and light into their works. When not giving inspiration to creators, he takes to relaxing in the sun, basking in the rays of its warmth and beauty while sharing them with those around him.

**Anathema** Pretend to be someone that you’re not, prevent someone from taking time for themselves, refuse to listen to both sides of an argument

**Area of Concern**
Rainbows, relaxation, self-worth, shade

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Lorris](/Deities.aspx?ID=477)
(The Savior Hound)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 308

For those who have lost everything, or who have never had it to lose, even the smallest kindness can mean the world. Lorris and his followers work to create these kindnesses, knowing that they can elevate the attitude of the most downtrodden. This can be as little as carrying candies in their pockets to share with those they meet, or larger gestures such as opening their homes to those who would not have a place of their own to safely lay their heads. Some even choose to go through the process of adopting orphans, welcoming them into their family as their own. Lorris has the appearance of a man with dark skin with a white hound’s head and clad in a simple beggar’s robe that never seems to dirty. He’s also known to take on the form of a droopy-eyed hound dog who drops off small meals to starving street children, rather than allowing them to continue begging for scraps.

When not serving the poor and destitute, Lorris works in consort with [Pharasma’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458) to find those who have passed after lives of suffering, and advocating for them to move directly onto the plane of Nirvana. With what they have endured upon the mortal plane, he believes that they have already paid for any transgressions and works to make sure that those who bear judgment are aware as well. Once these souls reach their new home, he ensures that they find their heart’s purest desire, small or large as it may be. He is most delighted when the spirit of a child asks for a puppy of their own, laughing as he ensures they have a loyal immortal companion.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Give to those less fortunate than you, help orphans find safe housing, assist those in need

**Anathema** Ignore the needs of others when they ask for your help, take from widows or orphans, contribute to the destruction of housing for the unfortunate

**Area of Concern**
Charity, the disadvantaged, orphans, volunteering

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Neshen](/Deities.aspx?ID=478)
(Knight of the Steel Lash)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 308

Neshen, Knight of the Steel Lash, is a loving but harsh god, demanding that those who come to him show they have truly changed through the trials he gives them. More hands on with the fate of his followers than many other gods, Neshen is known to manipulate situations to ensure that those who have turned to him really do plan to turn away from their previous lives. This can be as simple presenting them a temptation of the flesh, placing them on the receiving end of a fight, or even having them imprisoned for that which they have done before. Neshen doesn’t believe that change should be easy, but meaningful. Through meaningful change, his worshippers are those who tread closest to the light. 

Neshen appears as a battle-scarred [elven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=60) man with metallic skin. His skin bears particular segmentation and shaping, causing it to resemble living armor. When not working to facilitate the change of his followers, Neshen can be found working on the perfection of himself. Sometimes this is physical, through running or assisting others with manual labors; while other times it is mental, with reading the texts of ancient scribes, or even joining them in the tedious work of copying holy texts from their copies in the Great Library of Harmonious Scripture in Heaven. No matter what he is doing, he dedicates his whole self to it, leading his followers not only in word but also in deed.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Lead others to the light of good, strive for self-perfection, be a support for others struggling to change their ways

**Anathema** Stop taking care of yourself, wallow in self-pity, refuse to right past wrongs.

**Area of Concern**
Penitence, repentance, suffering

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Olheon](/Deities.aspx?ID=479)
(The Just Arbiter)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 308

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) This deity grants [_subconscious suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1692) instead of glimmer of charm_

Whether it be the rule of a king or queen over their nation, or that of parents with their children, Olheon judges all who hold positions of power over others. The Just Arbiter is said to observe all legitimate power relationships as her purview, showing favor to those who commit their responsibilities with righteousness. To those seeking her aid who are deserved of such, she works subtly, teaching through subjects who have enjoyed her favor in the past and her divine servitors. Tyrants and others who misuse their legitimate authority to oppress or disenfranchise those under their auspices cause her to boil. Woe be to the one whose actions cause her to descend from Heaven, herself to address the issue.

An awe-inspiring sight to behold, Olheon towers above most mortals at a height of over 16 feet, with brilliant blue eyes gleaming like sapphires in the dawn’s light. Her burnished gold skin is clad in silver plate armor with golden accents and rondelles. Her gauntlets, by contrast, are stained a bright crimson color. A surcoat of white and gold often tops this attire. The Just Arbiter bears a golden seal of office, and she carries a ruby-bladed halberd nearly one and a half times her own height, its every artistically sweeping curve sharpened to razor-sharpness.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Rule with honor, guard the weak and oppressed, obey righteous laws

**Anathema** Oppress others, fail to challenge tyrants or unjust laws, break a sworn promise

**Area of Concern**
Deservedness, nobility, rulership

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Pulura](/Deities.aspx?ID=480)
(The Shimmering Maiden)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 308

Dancing through the northern sky, the Shimmering Maiden represents constellations, homesickness, and the northern lights. Pulura understands that the constellations are the guide with which mortals navigate their world, lighting the way so that those who travel or explore can always find their way home. The stories of the constellations not only entertain with tales of goodness and light and strength, but they are also a tool for helping others learn and remember the stars. This ensures that the map in the sky is easy to read and never forgotten. To learn this map and to guide those who are lost or otherwise in need of direction are exceptional callings, but even more so is to teach a petitioner to navigate the skies themself on the journey. 

The Shimmering Maiden is often difficult to see, appearing to mortals from great distances as a bright, hot star. When she can be seen, she has the appearance of a Tian woman whose dark hair twinkles with bright stars. Her green and pink robes appear as though they are made of light, rather than any fabric. Travelers, guides, nomads, explorers, sailors, stargazers, and hunters are some of Pulura’s followers.

Constellations, homesickness, northern lights

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Ragathiel](/Deities.aspx?ID=481)
(General of Vengeance)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

The General of Vengeance presides over chivalry, duty, and vengeance, acting as the quintessential knight. Born of the [archdevil](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=4) [Dispater](/Deities.aspx?ID=66) and Feronia, a demigoddess of fire, Ragathiel struggles to overcome the reputation of his parentage, and he understands the struggle to be accepted, to be trusted, and to fight against his own nature for the sake of good. He represents strength in battle, wrath upon the wicked, absolution or vengeance for the wronged, leadership when needed, and virtue and duty to the innocent. He expects his followers to destroy [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) when they find them and to work toward truly earning the trust and acceptance of those around them. Those who follow him lead by shining example and can be found on the front lines of battle or any conflict against evil they can find. 

The General of Vengeance appears as a massive giant, standing more than 20 feet tall, clad in golden plate armor that shines with its own light and carrying a sword that burns with holy fire. Five flaming wings stretch from his back, three on his left and two on his right—the sixth was lost, torn out by his father in a fit of fury. Followers of Ragathiel include crusaders, knights, soldiers, the falsely accused, the marginalized, and the wronged.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Reymenda](/Deities.aspx?ID=482)
(Lady of Sacrifice)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Reymenda appears as an older woman, just past middle age. Depending on the tale, the Lady of Sacrifice once had children and lost them, or she found that her life was one where having children did not fit her needs and desires. Regardless of the reason, all know that Reymenda now swears an oath to never have children and expects the same of her followers. In exchange for this vow, Reymenda grants her followers the guidance and might they need to bear the responsibility of virtue. While some set out to strike down evil, others rally a community in the wake of a natural disaster, or do something as simple as helping a family by working in their kitchen for an evening when the family is too overburdened by life to cook a meal. No genuinely helpful responsibility is too small for a follower of Reymenda, though they know how to teach others to help themselves or work together, and they also do their best to avoid exploitation when someone grows careless and irresponsible. Reymenda teaches her followers to move on when it appears that others are taking advantage of a follower’s generosity. 

In addition to responsibilities, Reymenda focuses on endings. Life is full of moments of finality; Reymenda’s followers serve both as instigators of endings and counselors when endings occur. When facing evil, Reymenda teaches to be decisive, stopping evil as soon as one can. When dealing with other endings, such as the death of another or the end of a relationship, Reymenda teaches acceptance. Her followers do their best to help others through these times. Some followers do so by sharing advice, while others serve to take on responsibilities of others who can’t act during times of grief. Reymenda also teaches that although endings are conclusive, they can’t lead to new beginnings. The end of a relationship could lead to the individuals finding others elsewhere, or it could lead to the two reuniting some time later when they have grown into new people. 

Although Reymenda demands her followers remain childless, she does not abhor children. In fact, many of her teachings include the protection of children. Reymenda asks her followers to undertake dangerous missions and a lack of children makes these tasks easier. It’s not uncommon for her followers to eventually choose to have children. Reymenda encourages this, but no longer grants her blessings to these followers, instead asking them to find a different deity. Reymenda of all gods is used to these endings.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Bring definitive ends to known evils, consider genuine requests for aid, sacrifice when others can’t

**Anathema** Adopt or have a child, directly undo the death of another or the ending of something, treat children as a burden or a mistake, withhold news of another’s ending

**Area of Concern**
Childlessness, endings, responsibility

</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>
<title level="2">Devotee Benefits</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**[Divine Attribute](/Backgrounds.aspx?ID=132)**
Constitution or Wisdom

**Divine Font** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)

**Divine Sanctification**  can choose holy

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Seramaydiel](/Deities.aspx?ID=483)
(Lady of Inspired Notes)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

It is said that the song of Seramaydiel’s harp can calm the soul of the broken hearted, strengthen those headed into righteous battle, and lure evildoers into uncertain death. A battle-hardened warrior in her own right, she much prefers to solve conflicts with conversation instead of confrontation, for a soul lost in battle is one less soul that can create beautiful things. However, she will not shy away from fighting for what is right, and her songs are known to bolster those charging forth on a battlefield. 

When not worrying about solving conflicts, Seramaydiel prefers to spend her time creating beautiful music for all to hear. Her songs burst forth from her amphitheater home, filling the air of Nirvana for all who care to listen. The Lady of Inspired Notes puts all she can into her creations, using them to bring joy to the souls and lords that fill this plane of existence. It is even said that her music can be heard in other realms by those that worship her.

She does not keep her skill to herself, as she is more than willing to teach others to bring forth joy through their own instruments, both simple and complex. Her own worshippers will often call upon her when learning new and tricky combinations of musical sounds, hoping to draw inspiration and encouragement from her talents to play on their own instruments. While she generally offers indirect inspiration, she sometimes appears before her followers to offer guidance, taking the form of a dark-skinned [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=64) woman with curly, red-gold hair and white wings.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Create music in public where strangers can hear and be inspired by you, compose original poetry about that which you are passionate about, support the arts in any way you can

**Anathema** Destroy musical instruments, disparage others who create music, encourage others to not support artisans

**Area of Concern**
Communication, inspiration, music

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Soralyon](/Deities.aspx?ID=484)
(The Mystic Angel)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Guardians, monuments, and magic are the purview of the Mystic Angel. Soralyon believes that monuments and ruins are important links to the past. Guarding them against those who would defile or destroy them is of tremendous importance, lest people lose memories of who they are, where they came from, and what they have accomplished. As such, those who stand guard over monuments and ruins are blessed. Likewise, those who study monuments to the past and present with respect, or seek to preserve them for the future, are also blessed. Guardianship of others, but especially of those who study and preserve these relics to the past, is also of great importance to Soralyon. As magic is a valuable tool in guarding, studying, and preserving these monuments and people, Soralyon encourages the study of magic, preferably for use in learning or protection. 

When the Mystic Angel appears to mortals, he usually does so as a handsome being beautifully carved of smooth marble, the hues of which range the entire spectrum of the stone but tend toward those most common locally. Followers of Soralyon include archaeologists, historians, bodyguards, guardians, sculptors, curators, and arcane spellcasters.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Study monuments, craft golems and artwork from stone, guard ancient sites

**Anathema** Defile sacred buildings, knowingly unearth evil monuments, destroy historical artifacts

**Area of Concern**
Guardians, magic, monuments

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Uskyeria](/Deities.aspx?ID=485)
(The Saintly Slumberer)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Whether consciously or not, every successful hunter knows that a balance must be struck between observation and action, preparation and instinct, life and death. Taking more from the land than what is sustainable entices predators to be bolder, possibly turning the hunter into the hunted. Those who seek harmony while they live off the land give deference to Uskyeria, who urges her followers to embrace this equilibrium. Death is a natural part of life. Predators hunt prey. Those who are best suited to do so will survive. Those who don’t still feed the circle of life.

Appearing as a gigantic red [bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=370) with gold-streaked fur, Uskyeria is said to have originated in the far north of Avistan, between the realms currently known as Irrisen and the Sarkoris Scar. Also known as the Saintly Slumberer, this [agathion](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=216) empyreal lord’s invisible servitors bring restful sleep to both predator and prey, so they can awake refreshed upon the morn to begin the cycle anew. Insomniacs far from nature who seek balance in their own lives also pray to Uskyeria. Seeing their hunt for strength and balance just as deserving, she sometimes blesses them with the rest they so desperately need.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Preserve and protect natural resources, respect the animals you kill and their spirits, sleep as long as you need to in order to have strength for the day

**Anathema** Kill animals for reasons other than self-defense or sustenance, over-hunt the land, prevent others from getting as much sleep as they want

**Area of Concern**
Hunting, insomniacs, prudence, slumber

</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Valani](/Deities.aspx?ID=486)
(The Fireshaker)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Some things are constantly changing, and Valani teaches that an island is the greatest example of major changes. An island can be born from nothing, rising up from boiling waters. Over time, the hardened volcanic rock transforms into an island full of life. A powerful storm or more volcanic activity can wipe away all this life, leaving the island again bare for new change and transformation. Valani teaches embracing these changes and taking them in stride. The only way to find joy is to take each moment for what it is, and embrace all the changes that come. Valani holds great power over natural forces like storms and especially volcanoes. As such, he sees worship from [druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=34), elementalists, and people living on islands vulnerable to major changes from such primal forces. 

Valani himself is a massive [boar](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=372)-headed figure who wears a crown of spiked grass and purple flowers. His muscular body is encased in armor made from natural fibers that’s as tough as star-forged adamantine. Fireshaker is a creature of great emotional range. It is said that his laughter is strong enough to make tropical trees dance and give up their fruits to those in need, but his quickly found rage is so full of wrath that it can cleave peninsulas from their shores and cause island volcanoes to erupt for months on end. When pleased, his natural forces avoid destroying settlements or majorly disrupting lives. When upset, his storms topple homes and volcanic eruptions destroy hunting grounds, but never to such a degree that mortals can’t adapt to a new life, growing and thriving afterward.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Encourage change for the sake of rebirth, respect the power of the elements, focus on your own self growth

**Anathema** Refuse to accept changes in yourself or others, repress you own anger or laughter, use the forces of nature for wanton destruction

**Area of Concern**
Change, growth, primal forces, volcanic islands

**Pantheons**

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Vildeis](/Deities.aspx?ID=487)
(The Cardinal Martyr)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

The Cardinal Martyr presides over devotion, sacrifice, and scars. Vildeis is driven entirely by her abhorrence for evil. Evil should not and must not be allowed to exist. It should be fought without pause and without rest until it is completely and utterly destroyed. Not only does the fight never end, but every sacrifice that needs to be made to end evil must be made, and is worth making. There are no material rewards to be had for destroying evil—its destruction is all the reward necessary, and that reward can be enjoyed only once there is no more evil. Scars are the marks the fight leaves behind, the memory of all the sacrifices made. Vildeis expects total commitment from her followers, who leave everything behind and dedicate their lives to destroying all that is vile.

The Cardinal Martyr appears as a [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=64) woman covered in scars, each forming a rune depicting a sacrifice she has made. Her eyes are covered in a red cloth so that she cannot see the horrors of evil upon the world. Vildeis flies on gigantic, blood-colored wings, and the whispered screams of all the planes’ martyrs can be heard in her presence. Followers of Vildeis include martyrs, [champions](/Classes.aspx?ID=58), and zealots.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

**Edicts** Sacrifice yourself in pursuit of good, champion noble causes, scar your body

**Anathema** Joke or laugh about injustice, sacrifice others in your place, indulge in luxury

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Ylimancha](/Deities.aspx?ID=488)
(Harborwing)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Ylimancha gazes out over the world’s coastlines, presiding over coastal waters, fishers, and flying creatures. Also known as Harborwing, she believes in harmony between the seas, the skies, and the beings of the land. Sustenance and resources can be taken from the sea by those on shore, but not so much that the balance is upset. The sea may sometimes encroach on the land, but breakwaters can be built to keep communities on land dry and safe. Ylimancha likewise loves all creatures that fly, though the demon lord [Pazuzu](/Deities.aspx?ID=79) also claims dominion over them, bringing the two into endless conflict and causing her to mourn deeply each creature he converts to his worship. 

Harborwing most often appears as a very large seagull, pure white in color, with the head of a Varisian woman with short, dark hair. She sometimes instead appears as a human woman with the head of an osprey. In this form she wears robes that sway like the waves of the sea, their blue folds foaming white at the edges, and she carries a teak longbow strung with gold. When she speaks, her voice carries the sound of the crashing waves. Followers of Ylimancha include fishers, sailors, [merfolk](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=74), those who make their living on or in the sea, and flying creatures.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Empyreal Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=10)

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Zohls](/Deities.aspx?ID=489)
(Verity)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Zohls, also known as Verity, advocates for determination, investigation, and truth. She believes that mysteries lead to truth, but how one arrives at that truth is as important as the answer itself. All questions are puzzles, and all puzzles are worth solving. The more intricate the problem, the more rewarding the investigation, and through determination and ethical investigation, even the deepest of enigmas can be solved. Calculated, logical thinking is more important to Zohls than gut instinct. She sees patterns everywhere, and she teaches that detecting these patterns brings the truth to light. While all investigations are worth pursuing so long as they don’t hurt innocents, investigations that reveal the truth of crimes or other horrors, and that lead to justice being served and victims finding peace, are the most important of all to Verity.

When appearing to her followers, Verity is always practically dressed, usually wearing a tunic and breeches. She wears black and white clothing, as these colors represent the light of truth and the darkness of obscurity and the unknown. She keeps her light-brown hair pulled back in braids. On her back she has large wings made of parchment that never wrinkle, whether furled or unfurled. Followers of Zohls include detectives, scientists, researchers, historians, archivists, librarians, and even conspiracy theorists.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Aegirran](/Deities.aspx?ID=490)
(The Sea Dreamer)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) This deity grants [_illusory scene_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570) instead of dreaming potential_

Predominantly worshipped by tide giants, Aegirran is venerated as the god of dreams, sailing, and ocean voyages. Despite being wed to [Skode](/Deities.aspx?ID=239), he is forever separated from her except for during the brief changing of seasons when his coral-covered ship touches upon the shore. For the rest of the year, he is leading nine daughters (the gentle yet free east, south, and west winds) he bore with his wife on an endless journey across and below the seas, bringing rain and sunshine to the seaboard and inland fields. A hopeless romantic, he watches over the oceans. Most giant art depicts Aegirran as a tide giant, a giant with pale-blue skin and several markings reminiscent of waves. He is clad in a simple, asymmetrical cloak and sailing attire. 

While some worshippers of the Sea Dreamer might never cross a body of water in their lifetime, most of his followers are seafarers and the families that they leave behind. Before particularly long voyages, his followers often break off a small, insignificant piece of the vessel they intend to sail on, carve his religious symbol into it, and cast it into the sea. This is to let Aegirran know about their upcoming venture and to ask for his guidance and protection. In return, he can grant them this as well as dreams of their loved ones so that they do not have to suffer the heartache of being away from them.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Edicts** Sail the seas, offer a place for weary travelers to rest their heads, love and respect those you’re in relationships with

**Anathema** Betray a loved one’s trust, kill someone while they sleep, purposely pollute natural bodies of water

**Area of Concern**
Dreams, sailing, voyages

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Bergelmir](/Deities.aspx?ID=491)
(Mother of Memories)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Bergelmir is primarily worshipped by [cloud](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3015) and [stone giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3012), though many humans in the Realm of the Mammoth Lords have devoted themselves to her as well. She is related to [Skrymir](/Deities.aspx?ID=496), but the manner in which they are family constantly differs depending on the tale being told and its storyteller. Some say they are siblings, others say they are cousins, and just as many interpret them as mother and son. Regardless, as the goddess of family, elders, and genealogy, Bergelmir chronicles the lives and lineages of giant families and records their births and deaths in a colossal tome. Any histories, songs, stories, or rituals passed on by mouth or written word is remembered and immortalized by her, save for very few exceptions.

Sharing these records has always been vital to building community and camaraderie among giants while imparting vital wisdom to the people. Depending on the worshipper, the Mother of Memories appears as either a cloud or  stone giant, but always as an elderly woman with long hair kept in a tight braid, wearing a flowing dress, and carrying her family tome. Her followers tend to support, work at, or run libraries and other places of learning, marking their entrances or important records with her religious symbol: three lightning bolts representing history, ritual, and genealogy.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Edicts** Learn the traditions and history of your people, care for the elderly, share your wisdom and knowledge

**Anathema** Destroy historical texts or records, alter or obfuscate oral histories, harm the elderly

**Area of Concern**
Elders, family, genealogy, memories, tradition

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Fandarra](/Deities.aspx?ID=492)
(The Blood Mother)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Fandarra (sometimes bearing the title of the Blood Mother, the Earth Mother, or Mother of All) is the giant god of blood, the cycle of life, knowledge, and fertility. Followers claim that all animals, all people, and all deities were born from her. Though most acts of piety in her name are performed by [stone giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3012), many other types of giants, and even humans in cold climates like northern Avistan, worship her in some way, shape, or form as well. In largely giant followings, she is portrayed as a bald stone giant woman, her head adorned with a lush crown of leaves and flowers. Draped on her shoulders is a cloak of red fur, usually [mammoth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2994), [bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=370), or [wolf](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=485), and around her neck is a necklace of knives or arrowheads to bear against those who violate the natural cycles of life. When depicted in relation to fertility, she is also shown as pregnant, occasionally carrying an infant giant and bear in either arm. There are many tales of Fandarra in regard to her combat prowess, taking up arms to protect kin and the sanctity of life.

Fandarra’s worshippers value balance and care deeply for family, regardless of if they’re related by blood. Clanmates, friends, and even pets are considered family by followers of Fandarra. Most of her ecclesiastics are [clerics](/Classes.aspx?ID=33) and [druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=34), donning her religious symbol of a crescent moon rising up from behind a mountain.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Edicts** Abide by the cycles of life, aid childbirth, destroy undead

**Anathema** Strive to break the cycle of life (such as by attaining immortality or creating undead), despoil the earth, kill a juvenile creature

**Area of Concern**
Blood, the cycle of life, fertility, knowledge

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Haggakal](/Deities.aspx?ID=493)
(Father Moon)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Haggakal is an [ogre](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=451) god, the offspring of a profane deity and a giant, the names of which have been either lost or intentionally scrubbed from the annals of time and even [Bergelmir’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=491) tome, likely by the very beings who owned them. His hideous appearance is emphasized in all sorts of tales about him, regardless of whether the goal of the stories were to praise or disparage him. He is known as the god of ogres, darkness, illicit relationships and illicit deals among the few giants and even fewer ogres who worship him. His followers likewise become more unsightly the longer and more fervently they revere him. The only traits of Haggakal that are highlighted more than his unpleasant looks are his unholy strength and incredible cunning.

Many practices of Father Moon’s followers revolve around the phases of the moon. The waning moon means Haggakal has turned his back to the world to indulge in his more unsavory areas of concern, to incite the breaking of promises and bonds by offering people their deepest desires. He might proffer a newlywed, for example, with financial stability and, in return, ask for unfaithfulness to their spouse; he can tempt someone who has been wronged with the strength needed to enact revenge and, in return, ask for them to violently revolt against the benevolent leadership of their town. Haggakal takes great pleasure in the guilt and chaos born from these types of deals and encourages his followers to similarly sow discord during this lunar phase. The waxing moon signals Haggakal’s return and a facade of civility along with him.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Edicts** Always accept a challenge of strength, facilitate chaos and instability

**Anathema** Flee from battle, get married, hide the ugly features bestowed onto you by Haggakal

**Area of Concern**
Darkness, ogres, temptation

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Minderhal](/Deities.aspx?ID=494)
(He Who Makes and Unmakes)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Minderhal is both praised and scorned for his inflexibility and dedication to the status quo. The stone giant god of creation, giants, justice, and strength has a dwindling following, partially due to modern [stone giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3012) preferring to pay homage to the spirits of their ancestors over any gods. He instilled the belief that it is more honorable to exile yourself from a clan than to challenge superiors or existing traditions. Minderhal even went as far as encouraging the stone giants to accept being enslaved by the Thassilonians in exchange for political and magical might for himself, citing that it was only natural for the strong to subjugate the weak. Unsurprisingly, the moment the Thassilonian empire crumbled, so did faith in him. 

While other stone giant deities gracefully accepted similar waning of faith, He Who Makes and Unmakes did not. He has an intense detestation for those who turn their backs on him, while deeply cherishing the few who remain as if they are the fruits of his own artisanry. The majority of those who do continue to praise him are stone giants. Minderhal is depicted as an elderly, powerfully built stone giant wielding a magical crafting hammer named Weight of Tradition that returns to his hands when thrown. Followers magnify his name by erecting large statues of his image carved out of soft stone in front of places of leadership or by etching his religious symbol into their walls.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Edicts** Strictly follow established tradition, conquer the weak, obey those in power

**Anathema** Break tradition, question those with greater positional power, provide succor to those who have turned their backs on Minderhal

**Area of Concern**
Creation, giants, justice, strength

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Skode](/Deities.aspx?ID=495)
(The Horizon Huntress)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Skode, the daughter of [Tjasse](/Deities.aspx?ID=498), is the huntress goddess of giants. She is portrayed as a stout and muscular cloud giant woman with her face and arms decorated with intricate tattoos, dressed in leathers and furs. She is well-known for her skills with a longbow and spear, and her intolerance for evil. Skode is wed to [Aegirran](/Deities.aspx?ID=490), her might and passion having won his heart, but is separated from him by land and sea. The two are only ever able to embrace one another on the changing of seasons. For the remainder of the year, she forever stalks the land with the three sons she had with Aegirran (the bitter north winds), hunting the finest animals while seeking to destroy the wicked with arrows or spears aimed true. Like [Fandarra](/Deities.aspx?ID=236) and [Bergelmir](/Deities.aspx?ID=237), she is one of the few giant deities who have a significant following of non-giants as well, largely among the Kellid people in the Realm of the Mammoth Lords. 

Though a fair amount of her followers are hunters and the clans who rely on them for food, a great portion are young adult giants beginning coming-of-age rites, or those attempting to earn the respect or even the affection of another. A devotee of the Horizon Huntress can often be identified by the presence of her religious symbol on their hunting equipment, or by taking note of the game they hunt, as ardent followers only ever have the strongest of prey in their sights.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Edicts** Take the finest or healthiest specimen among any animals you hunt, respect your prey, protect your community, show bravery in the face of danger

**Anathema** Waste the animals you hunt, show cruelty to animals, abandon your community in a time of danger

**Area of Concern**
Heroism, hunting, pride, tracking, trophies

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Skrymir](/Deities.aspx?ID=496)
(The Seeker of Sunsets)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

As the trickster god of the [cloud giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3015), Skrymir is the very embodiment of wanderlust, mischievousness, and acuity. Nearly always masquerading as a new persona whenever he’s seen, he meanders the lands, rewarding those who correctly answer his riddles and pass his tests of wit. He has a familial connection with [Bergelmir](/Deities.aspx?ID=237), but whether it is as her brother, cousin, or son differs from myth to myth, likely due in no small part to his love of disguises and lighthearted chicanery. Because of his tendency to change his appearance and presentation at a whim, frequently slipping into whatever form feels most comfortable at that moment in time, there are no consistent depictions of him. He could be a frail, blithering old man one moment, then a strong inquisitive young woman the next. He is always a giant of some kind, and the excited glint in his eye when he meets someone with sharp wit and a curious mind seems to carry over regardless of the form he takes.

Followers of the Seeker of Sunsets tend to be travelers themselves, looking to him for both guidance and enjoyment on their journeys. Tales of his adventures and puckish trials are especially popular among younger giants, validating and encouraging their urge to explore and wander. Some worshippers can display his religious symbol on the soles of their footwear, but the best and most popular way to honor him is to indulge in the spirit of harmless inquiry and taking pleasure in the journey just as much as the destination.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Edicts** Seek out and explore unfamiliar places, use wit over violence whenever possible, lift spirits with harmless jokes and pranks

**Anathema** Settle down in one place for too long, harm someone with your jokes or pranks, turn down the chance to solve a riddle

**Area of Concern**
Riddles, wanderlust, wit

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Thremyr](/Deities.aspx?ID=497)
(The First Jarl)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Thremyr is a mountainous, ancient being with a long, full beard and hair of salt, a stout body of frigid ice, and a set of chilling blue gems for eyes. The earliest available records of the god tell the tale of his awakening during Earthfall as the meteorite landed on [Golarion](/Rules.aspx?ID=3298), tearing open the Inner Sea. Anything prior to that is currently undocumented or lost as a result of this cataclysmic event.

The First Jarl’s following is comprised almost entirely of frost giants. They claim that he is their unwitting creator, born of the glaciers and icebergs that sloughed off his body when he fell into the Steaming Sea. Initially, he saw these frost giants as small and unflattering mockeries of himself and chased them away. However, they eventually returned with tributes and praise for him. It was then that he finally decided to accept these frost giants as his own.

Though Thremyr is usually in a deep slumber, his followers go to great lengths to keep him happy, conducting raids on cities and villages to give the valuables they plunder to him as tributes. Altars and buildings used to store these ill-gotten gains are always distinctly marked with the image of a frozen blue gemstone. It’s well known and accepted by even his worshippers that he cares very little for them. It’s Thremyr’s love of treasures that keep them in his good graces, blessing those who consistently give great bounties with answered prayers and potent ancient magic.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Edicts** Take any valuables you come across, fiercely defend your treasure, never back down from a fight

**Anathema** Show anyone in your command favoritism, give up any of your own treasures, take the Test of the Starstone

**Area of Concern**
Ice, salt, tribute

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Tjasse](/Deities.aspx?ID=498)
(Lord of Talons)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Tjasse, the father of [Skode](/Deities.aspx?ID=239), is the [cloud giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3015) god and lord of rocs, wind, and high mountain peaks. He is credited for taming rocs to serve cloud giants and possessing shapeshifting abilities second only to [Skrymir](/Deities.aspx?ID=496) (much to his chagrin). Stories of his incredible arrogance precede him and most of his accolades, closely followed by cautionary tales of those unfortunate enough to have provoked his ire. The only thing that surpasses his vanity is his love for Skode, and the only thing greater than the wrath incurred by those who cross him is the destructive force he unleashes upon those who wrong his daughter. The Lord of Talons is a colossal being, dwarfing many members of the giant pantheon in comparison. He is always described as strikingly beautiful, though the exact details of his appearance change frequently.

Followers of Tjasse are often those who call the mountains their home, as well as roc tamers and riders who look to him to ensure the winds are in their favor when they take flight. There are a few who turn to him when seeking power to exact revenge, trusting that he understands and shares their need for decisive retaliation.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Edicts** Only ever hunt birds for food, aggressively oppose those who do not have your best interest at heart, protect your children at all cost

**Anathema** Harm birds for sport or via negligence, bow down to your oppressor, show mercy to anyone who wronged your child

**Area of Concern**
Giant birds, mountain peaks, pride

<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Urazra](/Deities.aspx?ID=499)
(Breaker of Bones)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 310

Faith in Urazra is relatively new, only coming about within the past few centuries. However, his following has grown rapidly among the young [stone](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3012) and [marsh giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3011) of a more violent incline. Even in the short amount of time that he has been a part of the giant pantheon, there have already been countless tales of his barbarity. In all of them, he is described as a blood-splattered stone giant man with a burly build, pauldrons fashioned from deer or moose skulls, and a jagged onyx-black greatsword with a length that rivals his height.

Followers of the Breaker of Bones are known as the [Bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=370) Cult, a faith that is quickly and effectively spreading thanks to their charismatic preachers and barbarians claiming their powerful rage is from the god himself. Many stone giant warriors are among his worshippers, drawn in by his promise of unholy might, resilience, and an immunity to pain for those who enact ruthless violence and conduct sacrifices of flesh in his name. They tout ferocity and strength as aspects more important than the works, knowledge, or history that their elders taught them, causing the cult to be met with great enmity from other stone giants.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Edicts** Fight anyone who disrespects you, be in the front line of battle, fight to the death

**Anathema** Flee from battle, show mercy to your enemy, beg for mercy

**Area of Concern**
Battle, brutality, strength

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Yrmidar](/Deities.aspx?ID=500)
(The Old Bear)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 312

Yrmidar is a [werebear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3237) frost giant deity of shipbuilders, camaraderie, and the hunt. When in his giant form, he is depicted as a frost giant with long silver hair in a braid that reaches past the small of his back, snowwhite eyes, and sharp obsidian-black fingernails. In his hybrid form, which is his most common form, his head transforms into that of a polar bear and his fingernails grow to become a bear’s claws. In his werebear form, he becomes an enormous, bipedal polar bear with deadly fangs and claws. When he transforms into this beast, even the most battle-hardened of deities steer clear of him. Most mortals and gods alike consider this affliction a curse, but Yrmidar does not share that belief. To him and many of his followers, it is a power and a responsibility to be proud of and at peace with. 

Faith in Yrmidar is most popular among frost giant shipbuilders and hunters; however, he has garnered a significant following among the Ulfen people as well. Large settlements of the faithful tend to form near coastlines perfect for ports or forests ideal for hunting. They have such a tangible sense of togetherness between them that it can be rather unnerving for unaccustomed outsiders to witness. Yrmidar asks his followers to support one another, emphasizing the importance of community. His aphorism “Aid Happily, Accept Gratefully,” is often emblazoned beneath his religious symbol. Many hunting and sailing parties thank the Old Bear for helping bring them together, and in many cases, even directly leading to marriages.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Anathema** Be too proud to give or ask for help, intentionally provoke wild animals, choose yourself over your community

Camaraderie, the hunt, shipbuilders

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Zursvaater](/Deities.aspx?ID=501)
(Prince of Steel)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 312

Zursvaater is considered the patron deity of [fire giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3014). According to legend, well over ten millennia ago, a [stone giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3012) clan was scaling the rocky face of the mightiest volcano on Golarion, once known as the Molten Summit. He took on a new form to better communicate with them, molding the mountain range that was his body into a colossal humanoid figure of volcanic rock and ash. In this form, he called himself Zursvaater and offered the clan the secrets of fire and metal in exchange for their complete devotion and their very souls. Upon accepting, they were subsumed by the god before being expelled as beings of molten rock and flame—the first fire giants.

Zursvaater prefers to involve himself in the events of the mortal world rather than interact with other deities and divine beings. Worshipped by fire giants as the Prince of Steel, he is portrayed as a fearsome fire giant in armor and a closed-faced helm, with a heavy chain in one hand and, in the other, a red-hot [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) [greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=379) named _Blood of the Mountains_.

Zursvaater’s religious symbol can often be found as tattoos of gold or copper on the body of his clerics. The Prince of Steel strongly discourages inter-clan fighting among his fire giants, instead urging his followers to war with other peoples and pillage resources that would improve further war efforts. He abhors [Thremyr](/Deities.aspx?ID=497) for his tactless style of warfare, believes [Minderhal](/Deities.aspx?ID=494) pales in comparison to him, and begrudgingly acknowledges [Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=294) as a skilled smith but nothing more.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Giant Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=26)

**Edicts** Conquer the weak, devise plans ahead of anticipated battles, only bring wellmaintained weapons to fights

**Anathema** Engage in violent infighting, claim anyone but Zursvaater gives you your strength, disrespect a skilled smith

**Area of Concern**
Conquest, metal crafting, tactics

</column>

anathema: React poorly or offensively to mischief, silence loud noises
edict: Make yourself known, make fun of the unsuspecting
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Cong](/Deities.aspx?ID=502)
(The Smash-crash)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 312

No place where Cong appears remains calm. One can notice them from ten miles away due to their cacophonous presence. Cong, the Smash-crash, is one of the few goblin hero-gods who often presents themself in avatar form, and then only to those who have a great prank in mind and need an extra hand to carry it out. Cong’s appearance is that of a [goblin](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=422) with a multitude of trinkets attached to every available part of their body, each a different instrument for noise or pranking. They are mainly popular among children and the most chaotic of goblins. Followers of Cong are often frowned upon by other ancestries, but most goblin tribes endorse these unbridled acts and get a good chuckle out of it. Many more goblins are punished for reacting badly to one of Cong’s followers (or Cong themself) than punishment toward those who carry out such pranks. This is mostly because Cong’s pranks are never done with the intention of harming other individuals, but rather for everyone else to get a big laugh out of it (though being goblins, the harmless intentions do not always translate into harmless acts). 

Apart from causing mischief, Cong is a great fan of musical numbers, though their artistic sensibilities are notably goblin in nature. Followers of Cong make sure to detune their instruments or abuse them as hard as they can to make the most cacophonous noises possible. Their followers usually choose the path of the bard, even though their companions might not enjoy their presence. Congite adventurers are always hitting everything in their way with sticks to make percussive sounds on their journeys, trying their best to get others to join in.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Goblin Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=27)

**Edicts** Make yourself known, make fun of the unsuspecting

**Anathema** React poorly or offensively to mischief, silence loud noises

**Area of Concern**
Cacophonies, mischief, tricks

</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>
<title level="2">Devotee Benefits</title>

<column gap="tiny">

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Hadregash](/Deities.aspx?ID=503)
(Greatest Supreme Chieftain Boss)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 312

Hadregash, the foremost goblin creator-god, is often depicted as a [barghest](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=11) with muscles rippling under his smoke-colored fur. Known to be the strongest and mightiest among the four creator-gods, he instills the idea that in numbers there is strength, and strength equals power. Many goblinoids follow his creed and rulership by creating allegiances within the different tribes and paying as much respect as possible to the chain of command. [Hobgoblins](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=78) hold Hadregash in the highest regard, using his teachings as their founding principles: work in numbers; strategically plan your next ambush to harm enemies as little as possible, taking the greatest number of individuals as prisoners; and rule all lands with an iron fist.

The Greatest Supreme Chieftain Boss accepts many kinds of offerings from his followers (blood sacrifices being his favorite). Powerful weapons must be a close second, as he sends his most capable followers to retrieve these offerings from altars and use them in combat. Hadregash rarely sees worship among non-goblinoids, except in the case of cruel tyrants and similarly minded individuals.

**Anathema** Bow before others, let others control your actions, permit insubordination

**Area of Concern**
Conquest, invasion, war

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Lurlup](/Deities.aspx?ID=504)
(Big Eater Brother)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 312

Not much is known of Lurlup but that he has an endless appetite. Big Eater Brother appears as a typical [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=62) with dark blue skin and multiple mouths all around his body, such as the ones within his palms and on his lower back. He is a goblin hero-god revered by those who adore eating, though not necessarily because they enjoy the taste of food. Lurlup’s followers eat because they enjoy the act of eating, and feel the need to be chewing or having something in their mouth. This means that the two main things they value are a food’s texture and temperature. They also find purpose in eating unique things, such as parts from hydras or elementals. 

There is a common belief among Lurlup’s followers that by eating, one is not actually feeding oneself but Lurlup, who needs a constant supply of food. Followers say Lurlup is well-connected, and that he will pull strings with the other gods to make sure there is always food (or at least something for his followers to chew on) in reach. Additionally, goblin chefs (or “chieps,” as they call themselves), who tend to be the primary followers of Lurlup, are often granted blessings from their god to be able to produce more food out of the same amount of ingredients.

Some tribes of goblins, such as the Corpse Munchers, are so devoted to Lurlup that they go as far as to extirpate their own stomachs and undergo a ritual to allow Lurlup to create a new divine stomach in its place. This new stomach feeds Lurlup directly with the ingested food while still satiating the goblin’s hunger.

<column gap="tiny">

**Edicts** Feed upon everything in your surroundings

**Anathema** Be fussy with your food, choose one meal over another, waste a meal

**Area of Concern**
Chefs, feasts, hyperphagia

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Teki Stronggut](/Deities.aspx?ID=505)
(The Rememberer)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 312

Teki Stronggut surprised many scholars in Golarion when she suddenly ascended as a hero-god; most had never heard of her or knew only stories of her failures. Those who personally knew her, however, describe Teki as the kind of person who always ends up achieving her dreams, despite many setbacks and the obvious odds. [Goblins](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=62), [bugbears](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=374), and all kinds of unlikely adventurers were rooting for her, as she remembered and represented them all. “Everyone can become a hero if they put their mind to it,” or so Teki would put it. Teki appears much like she did in life: a goblin with bright yellow skin, a cloak with images of failed heroes she supported in her life, a variety of old weapons and other adventuring junk, and an unremovable grin.

Although often unappreciated by the populace, the Rememberer makes a great effort to have word of unsung heroes reach settlements throughout the Inner Sea. With this knowledge in hand, many of these settlements reach out for help from these unknowns when troubles arise. Her followers are mostly adventurers that get their start with Teki’s help. Once that first job is complete, a hero might gain just enough renown to gain further opportunities. Teki teaches these now-famed heroes to spread the word of other unknown heroes and help continue the chain of renown. Teki’s followers are usually members of guilds, such as monster-slayer guilds or thieves’ guilds, hoping to use the organizations to gain greater recognition. 

Teki Stronggut instructs her followers to leave old weapons, unneeded magic items, or other gear that might help aspiring adventurers start their journey on her altars. These altars tend to have guards to watch over these items, and those who manage to steal from a Stronggut Station earn Teki’s wrath. This wrath manifests as unknown warriors that chase thieves, unfortunate luck on journeys, or even a dwindling of a given thief’s reputation, making acquiring more work difficult. Only once the items are returned or items of similar value are left in their place does Teki let up.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Goblin Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=27)

**Edicts** Remember those whom others forget, help those who need it the most, aid other misfits in their goals

**Anathema** Give up on your cause, punish someone due to failure or incompetence, abandon a fellow adventurer

**Area of Concern**
Haplessness, heroism, the forgotten

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Venkelvore](/Deities.aspx?ID=506)
(Most Glorious Neverfull)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 312

Among [goblins](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=62), Venkelvore is considered the most beautiful of gods. Many goblins depict her as a fellow goblin rather than her true form: a [barghest](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=11) with pale blue fur and a trio of tails. Venkelvore has an eternal hunger that’s only satiated by consuming her enemies. She prefers to eat her meals while still alive, as she not only feeds on the meat but also the prey’s suffering, which Venkelvore considers a fantastic side dish. 

Among the many ways to venerate Venkelvore, her followers believe that the best way to impress her is by tying up as many prisoners as they can during raids and burning them alive, savoring them as they cook or as part of a celebration once the pillaging ends. Her high priests say that the screams of agony please Venkelvore, who rewards those who follow her with more teeth, or sharpening their teeth and claws, allowing these followers to eat their next meal with greater ease.

Goblins who venerate the Most Glorious Neverfull sometimes worship [Lurlup](/Deities.aspx?ID=504) as well. They dedicate their lives to eating everything they can, trying to satiate the hunger of both of their gods. These voracious individuals tend to live isolated lives, as most other goblins tend to find their habits off-putting.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Goblin Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=27)

**Edicts** Gain beauty through pain and effort, keep a meal within arms length, torment those you eat

**Anathema** Reject a gift of food, neglect your appearance, starve yourself

anathema: Befriend or spare a dog
edict: Learn mounted combat, perform arson, hunt and kill dogs
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Zarongel](/Deities.aspx?ID=507)
(The Bark Breaker)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 312

Zarongel is the creator-god of [dog](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=392) killing, fire, and mounted combat. That [barghests](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=11) are profoundly dogshaped seems not to matter to either Zarongel or his followers. By all accounts, Zarongel appears to despise domesticated canines for becoming subservient to mortals. He’s often depicted as a wolf-like barghest with his hair composed of burning fire. His mouth drips a magma-like substance that mimics molten saliva. 

While not the strongest of the four creator-gods, Zarongel is surely the most agile one, with strong legs ready to hunt. He trained goblins to hunt dogs, creating a set of tactics involving arson specifically designed to leave them no escape route. Many [goblins](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=62) pass these canine hunting methods down through generations. According to Zarongel, once slain, a dog must be burnt away to fully eliminate them. Zarongel’s followers aim to leave no trace behind, keeping the few remaining bones for rituals and stews.

Zarongel doesn’t heed every prayer. Anyone looking to gain his attention must first burn a hunted dog. Goblins believe that anyone attempting to commune with Zarongel before accomplishing this trial results in bad luck for one hundred months. Those who manage to communicate with the Bark-Breaker typically earn the gift of a riding companion, typically a goblin dog or warg. Zarongel’s faithful are expected to be able to ride such beasts, though the god offers them no aid in this endeavor, and more than a few unlucky goblins have been eaten by their own god-sent mounts.

<column gap="tiny">

Arson, hunt, ritual

edict: Ply the sea, work fervently and without help, use what is given to you
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Zogmugot](/Deities.aspx?ID=508)
(Lady Lastbreath)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 312

The brains of the four goblin creator-gods, Zogmugot is a goddess with a baleful temper and obsessive temperament. Depictions of Lady Lastbreath show her as a [barghest](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=11) with sea-green fur. Always watching with her colorful sea-glass eyes, [goblins](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=422) risk much by gaining her attention. She dislikes being bothered by mortal petitioners, and if she decides that a goblin should have figured out a problem on their own, she is quick to unleash her wrath. Zogmugot is in charge of deciding whether a goblin drowns in water or is washed back ashore, and she viciously drowns her victims when she’s in a bad mood. Most goblins ritually placate her for this reason, while followers desperate to get her attention go to the depths of the sea and attempt to drown themselves. 

Followers of Zogmugot who need her the least are rewarded with inspiration and materials for their inventions, washed ashore by the waves. This often leads to her followers becoming the inventors or weaponforgers of their tribes. By creating new inventions, they pay her homage and are rewarded with new ideas, which usually lead to new inventions, thus creating a neverending cycle. The deadlier the invention, the better the reward. If it’s some kind of torture mechanism that involves drowning their victims, all the better.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Goblin Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=27)

**Edicts** Ply the sea, work fervently and without help, use what is given to you

**Anathema** Unnecessarily seek divine attention or guidance, disrespect the water, bury a piece of sea glass

**Area of Concern**
Drowning, invention, self-sufficiency

</column>
