markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Nyarlathotep (The Crawling Chaos)](/Deities.aspx?ID=573)

</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 318

Nyarlathotep is a being of a thousand shapes, each of which has a name, such as the [Haunter of the Dark](/Deities.aspx?ID=222) or the [Veiled Voice](/Deities.aspx?ID=575). Because he has walked the world in mortal form, Nyarlathotep is unique among the Outer Gods for appearing comprehensible and understandable—but this is a facade. The Crawling Chaos, as he is also known, appears humanlike not because he identifies with mortal concerns or cares for his mortal followers, but because a mortal shape makes it easier for him to do his work: spreading the influence of the Outer Gods.

In his role as a herald, messenger, and agent provocateur for the Outer Gods, Nyarlathotep is the Outer God most likely to have direct interactions with mortals. He answers prayers, teaches evil magic, and inserts himself into history—all to enable mortal cruelty, facilitate the release of Great Old Ones, and turn whole societies toward worship of the Outer Gods. He was instrumental in the fall of Ancient Osirion, and some believe he led Taldor to launch the Armies of Exploration, a glorious folly that proved the ruin of that empire. There are countless tales of his interaction with (and often possession of) mortal cultists and magicians who dare to call upon him, and they all end in grotesque corruption and death. Nyarlathotep also lingers among [Azathoth’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=161) court, carrying out schemes under the guise of the Primal Chaos’s will.

<column gap="tiny">

**Area of Concern**
Conspiracies, dangerous secrets, forbidden magic

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Nyarlathotep (The Faceless Sphinx)](/Deities.aspx?ID=574)

</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 318

Followers of Nyarlathotep in the guise of the Faceless Sphinx act as his agents on the edges of political circles, influencing lawmakers—with words, bribery, or magic—to enact policies that further their master’s apocalyptic goals. On Golarion, the Faceless Sphinx was worshipped in ancient Thassilon before its fall as well as in Osirion, where machinations begun in that nation’s founding years remain in motion to this day. Further cults of the Faceless Sphinx can be found planted within governments across the Universe, as well as in the [Dreamlands](/Planes.aspx?ID=47) territory of Leng. Because the Faceless Sphinx urges his devotees to expand the congregation themselves, this form of Nyarlathotep is only rarely seen by mortals. Those unfortunate enough to have been visited by the Faceless Sphinx in person meet with an ashen-skinned, elephantine [sphinx](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=209) whose countenance can only be described as a fathomless void.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Outer Gods and Great Old Ones](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13)

**Edicts** Indoctrinate the desperate and needy, influence institutions of power to hasten the end of the world

**Anathema** None

**Area of Concern**
Conspiracies, political corruption, vengeance

</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Nyarlathotep (The Veiled Voice)](/Deities.aspx?ID=575)

</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 318

Throughout Golarion’s history, a great many mortals with ambitions above their station have “fortuitously” stumbled upon dangerous secrets that changed their life’s trajectory forever. In many such cases, this is the wicked work of the Veiled Voice, who seeds the fields of history in preparation for a final, deadly harvest. Those who intentionally pledge themselves to this aspect of Nyarlathotep do so in search of divine inspiration in their pursuit of scientific or magical knowledge, whether or not they understand that the Voice’s blessing manifests in discoveries that accelerate the end of all things. As the Veiled Voice, Nyarlathotep assumes the form of a semi-corporeal humanoid figure cloaked in a dreamlike haze, though he only physically appears this way when necessary; in most cases, he moves invisibly through [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) or takes on the form of an authority figure to engender trust in his listeners.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Outer Gods and Great Old Ones](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13)

**Edicts** Innovate destructive alchemy or magic, instigate conflict between your enemies, share dangerous secrets

**Anathema** None

**Area of Concern**
Dangerous secrets, long-term plans, reckless innovation

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Orgesh](/Deities.aspx?ID=576)
(The Faceless God)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 318

The first spawn to rise from the frigid, oily fluid that flows through the Land of Black Blood deep in [the Darklands](/Rules.aspx?ID=2993), Orgesh is the ravenous but cunning Great Old One of hunger and alchemy. It quickly outgrew its fellow children of the Black Blood, the charda, and departed without warning to pursue greater ambitions elsewhere in the Darklands. Nonetheless, many charda continue to worship their wayward ancestor, though just as many curse its name for what they perceive as abandonment in favor of a new kindred found deep within the darkness of the Sightless Sea. The Faceless God’s true form is that of a malformed humanoid with overlong clawed legs that stretch out from a grossly swollen belly, its head completely featureless except for a maw that hangs open to reveal countless serrated teeth.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Outer Gods and Great Old Ones](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13)

**Edicts** Surpass your peers in your chosen field at any cost, travel using subterranean waterways, use blood and toxic metals in alchemy

**Anathema** None

**Area of Concern**
Alchemy, caverns, hunger, subterranean waterways

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Rhan-Tegoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=577)
(Herald of the End Times)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 318

Rhan-Tegoth, last to sleep and first to wake, is a Great Old One tasked with being the forerunner of the end of all things. Many centuries ago, Rhan-Tegoth ruled a mighty empire on a far-flung world. When its subjects turned away from its rule, it did not retaliate; instead, it entered a hibernation state, transforming into a stone effigy. In the eons since, the Herald has amassed followers across [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) and beyond, each cult subtly guided by Rhan-Tegoth’s terrible influence. Though some of these groups seek to hasten their god’s awakening, such an act—in the rare case of its success—has invariably resulted in the devouring of all those involved before the Herald simply returns to its timeless slumber. 

One common thread among all of Rhan-Tegoth’s cults is their creation of stone images of the god. These statues depict a large insectoid being with six pincered limbs, a three-eyed head featuring a multi-pronged proboscis, and a bloated abdomen covered in bulbous growths. Because these idols are eerily similar both to each other and the Herald, it has quickly become impossible to ascertain which is the original and which are the images, a fact which further frustrates efforts to interfere with Rhan-Tegoth’s cataclysmic destiny.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Outer Gods and Great Old Ones](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13)

**Edicts** Create statues of Rhan-Tegoth, sacrifice living sentient creatures

**Anathema** None

**Area of Concern**
Hibernation, immortality, ruin

</column>

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Xhamen-Dor](/Deities.aspx?ID=578)
(The Inmost Blot)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 318

Xhamen-Dor, the living cancer, was born in the sewers beneath Carcosa, the alien city that is the home of [Hastur](/Deities.aspx?ID=162). Somehow, this Great Old One was transported to [Golarion](/Rules.aspx?ID=3298) within a comet, crashing into the bottom of a lake during the devastation of Earthfall. Physically, it manifests as a twisting and vaguely serpentine-shaped mass of bone, hair, and fungus.

The Inmost Blot, as Xhamen-Dor is also known, exists to conquer worlds by infecting the living with itself. Its victims, the seeded, enact its will and seek to spread its influence until the entire world is but an extension of Xhamen-Dor. Then, once only the seeded remain, Xhamen-Dor uses the last of the planet’s energy to return to Carcosa, which in turn absorbs the world Xhamen-Dor has brought to it, allowing Carcosa to slowly grow.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Outer Gods and Great Old Ones](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13)

Decay, parasites, transformation

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Yig](/Deities.aspx?ID=579)
(Father of Serpents)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 318

Known to mortals as the Father of Serpents, Yig is an outlier among Great Old Ones in that he appears, at least on the surface, to pose little threat to mortals. The truth of the matter is somewhat more complicated, however, as the serpentine god enters intermittent feeding frenzies, during which even his most devout followers know to avoid attracting his attention, lest they become his next meal. When appearing before his congregation, Yig often takes on the form of a massive serpentine being with arms and legs, covered in green scales except for a distinctive silver crescent on his brow.

Worship of the Father of Serpents is isolated almost exclusively to rural communities that rely on seasonal cycles, such as nomadic followings and farming villages, and as such very few temples exclusively dedicated to the serpent god exist. Instead, worshippers congregate in forest clearings and cave mouths to leave tribute to Yig, most often in the form of food. Despite his position as a god of [snakes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=473), [serpentfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=468) society treats Yig with irreverence as a result of his historical rivalry with the now-dead [Ydersius](/Deities.aspx?ID=323); Yig, meanwhile, pays this disdain no heed.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Outer Gods and Great Old Ones](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13)

**Edicts** Feast in the autumn, perform seasonal rituals, protect your home from trespassers

**Anathema** None

**Area of Concern**
Cycles, procreation, serpents

</column>

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Yog-Sothoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=580)
(Lurker at the Threshold)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 318

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) This deity grants [_true target_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1726) instead of time beacon_

Yog-Sothoth is, along with [Azathoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=161), one of the greatest of the Outer Gods. In appearance, he is said to be a congeries of iridescent spheres—brilliant, foaming bubbles that constantly expand and collapse in upon themselves. He has other manifestations, however, including the Lurker on the Threshold, a mass of black tentacles that endlessly writhe, reach, and grow, and a mysterious humanoid figure hidden behind a shimmering veil. 

Yog-Sothoth embodies all of space and time; he exists in all places and in all moments simultaneously. Paradoxically, however, he is unable to manifest in the mortal universe unless summoned, a magical act that almost always results in untold destruction. He is known as the Key and the Gate, and magicians and cults research him in an effort to master time and space. Fortunately, his worship is not widespread, but some of those who have delved deep into his secrets describe him as an ambivalent figure—rather than a malevolent one—who guards cosmic secrets and makes them accessible to those who dare to ask for them. Yog-Sothoth reveals the true nature of the universe, but this is a thing that once seen cannot be unseen, and wise mortals turn aside from Yog-Sothoth’s offer of cosmic knowledge in favor of a more mundane existence.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Outer Gods and Great Old Ones](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13)

**Edicts** Gather knowledge of gates through space and time, curse or mutate unborn children

**Anathema** None

**Area of Concern**
Gates, space, time

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Aonaurious](/Deities.aspx?ID=581)
(The Enigma Clot)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Aonaurious appears as a sleek insectile creature with an obsidian carapace and razor-sharp claws that drip with blood. A quartet of unblinking eyes are spaced evenly around his head so that he can see in all directions and dozens of legs provide him the speed to pursue any prey. Called the Enigma Clot, he is the most violently aggressive qlippoth lord, always ready to wade into battles against demons and mortals alike. He grants no quarter, expects none in return and will mercilessly chase any who flee from a fight until he can tear them to pieces.

For reasons only understood by him, Aonaurious has spent the past few centuries venting his wrath on worlds in distant star systems, only recently returning to Golarion when he learned of the Whispering Tyrant’s escape. He believes any resulting bloodshed will fuel his powers and perhaps even grant him new followers. In either case, it foreshadows the death of many mortals—a prospect in which the Enigma Clot delights.

Worshippers of this qlippoth lord are usually heartless mercenaries, gladiators, and hunters looking to increase their martial prowess by any means necessary. The truly devoted can continue to fight past when grievous wounds should lay them low and track prey for days on end without rest. Their fanaticism usually means they live short lives, either dying in battle or collapsing from overexertion. As such, membership in cults of Aonaurious often dwindles quite quickly before disappearing.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Qlippoth Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=30)

**Edicts** Spill blood, use any method necessary to kill, vent your wrath on any and all

**Anathema** Allow a quarry to escape, stanch a bleeding wound

**Area of Concern**
Bloodshed, tireless pursuit, wrathful combat

</column>

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Chavazvug](/Deities.aspx?ID=582)
(The Crawling Inferno)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Chavazvug is a towering mound of internal digestive organs supported by five long slender legs. Steam constantly rises from his grotesque body, and his touch can set creatures and objects ablaze. Chavazvug focuses on the destruction of [demonkind](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387). He appreciates the power of unholy fire to reduce even the most powerful enemies to mere dust. The Crawling Inferno has personally lead armies of his minions on invasions into demonic realms, knowing that, if slain, he can grow a new body from one of the many boiling lakes of bile within his cavernous realm.

Chavazvug cares little for any cultists devoted to him. Most worshippers of the Crawling Inferno are lone arsonists and pyromaniacs who simply enjoy seeing things burn and venerate Chavazvug in the hopes that one day, the qlippoth lord will grant them the ultimate reward of spontaneous combustion. Rarely, a group of demon hunters will dedicate themselves to Chavazvug to grow stronger in their never-ending fight. They often eventually go to extremes, torturing mortal demon worshippers with redhot pokers to learn more about a cult’s activities. When this eventually proves fatal, the devotees of Chavazvug sever the captive’s head and burn it on a pyre as an offering within a burned-out building or forest consumed by wildfires.

**Edicts** Commit arson, destroy demons, reduce your enemies to dust

**Anathema** Aid a demon, stop a fire from spreading

**Area of Concern**
Demon hunters, fiery consumption, monstrous recursion

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Isph-Aun-Vuln](/Deities.aspx?ID=583)
(The Feaster Within)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

When not within a mutated host body, Isph-Aun-Vuln manifests as an incorporeal mass of roiling tentacles arrayed around a toothy maw. However, few see this true form, as the Feaster Within uses subterfuge and other cunning methods to erode the free will of mortals. She works to remove their capacity for sin and, eventually, their lives. Isph-Aun-Vuln has a fascination with mortal religions as she sees many churches as the tools of other gods to control their followers. Unlike most other qlippoth lords, she actively seeks worshippers to exert her influence over. 

Cults to Isph-Aun-Vuln are usually led by charismatic priests whose minds are controlled by her. These leaders use deceptive methods of recruitment and indoctrination, slowly erasing the members’ capacity to think for themselves. Most devotees don’t even realize their newfound religion has anything to do with a qlippoth lord until after Isph- Aun-Vuln’s influence has permanently stained their souls. In addition to standard brainwashing techniques, such cults also employ diseases and poisons that sap the mind’s strength. They prey on the traumatized and, if an individual proves to be too strong-willed to succumb to the leader, they are usually sacrificed via a fall from a great height.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Qlippoth Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=30)

**Edicts** Allow wounds to fester, be as changeable as the winds, present the illusion of choice while imposing your will

**Anathema** Cure someone of a disease, let those under your command make their own choices

**Area of Concern**
Infestations of the flesh, poisonous winds, removal of free will

</column>

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Nyuo-Ogh](/Deities.aspx?ID=584)
(The Always Observing)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Nyuo-ogh appears as a floating lizard eye surrounded by a darkness from which small grasping claws appear to grab at anything in their reach. The Always Observing was recently born from one of Oaur-Ooung’s blisterwombs, making him the youngest qlippoth lord in existence. Nyuo-Ogh seeks to plunge mortalkind into literal and educational darkness, eradicating both science and magic. He believes this will radically shorten mortal life spans and capacity for sin, with the additional effect of erasing their understanding of the rituals used to contact demons. As part of this process, Nyuo-Ogh constantly watches the mortal planes, looking for ways to slow the advancement of knowledge.

Followers of Nyuo-ogh often become metaphorical windows for the qlippoth lord to peer out at mortalkind. The Always Observing might commandeer a devotee’s vision at any moment for a random amount of time. During this time, the worshipper still controls their own actions but is effectively blind. This partial possession is obvious as the cultist’s eyes shift to have diamond-shaped pupils surrounded by veins of blood. Despite this, certain people still worship Nyuo-Ogh because of the control it gives them over the dissemination of knowledge and magic. These individuals—often unethical teachers, trainers, and wizards—hope to rule over the ignorant, even if they are the ones who fostered this lack of education.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Qlippoth Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=30)

**Edicts** Kill other practitioners of magic, obliterate systems of education, watch your enemies and learn from them

**Anathema** Educate others, rely too much on magic

**Area of Concern**
Destruction of knowledge, eternal darkness, unraveling of magic

</column>

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Oaur-Ooung](/Deities.aspx?ID=585)
(The Blistering Womb)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Oaur-Ooung is a massive, pale fungoid creature covered with blisters that endlessly give birth to more qlippoths, fueling their ranks for the war against demons. Some of her spawn drown within her impossibly deep oceanic realm and some she consumes upon birth, but countless others escape to the wider [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45). The Blistering Womb is also surrounded by smaller leviathans that can survive the toxic waters that protect her from demonic intrusions. Scholars believe that Cyth-V’sug, a [qlippoth](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=460) who became a [demon lord](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=5), was spawned from one of her blisters. Most recently, she also gave birth to Nyuo-Ogh, one of the most recent qlippoth lords to rise.

The Blistering Womb’s cultists, who often congregate at the edge of poisonous waters or in shipwrecks, have a singular goal: to be reborn as qlippoths. They care little that such a transformation means the obliteration of everything they currently are. Devotions to Oaur-Ooung include drinking foul concoctions of poisons and waste liquids, swimming long distances through tainted waters, and ritual sacrifices of living creatures via drowning. Those who please Oaur- Ooung are granted the ability to craft hideous creatures from the severed pieces of their own bodies. This often results in nearby communities hunting down these newly created monsters. In the end, the souls of the worshippers of the Blistering Womb are sent to her realm for her to do with as she sees fit, usually consuming them instead of transforming them.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Qlippoth Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=30)

**Edicts** Embrace all manner of change, pollute pristine waters, practice fleshwarping on yourself and others

**Anathema** Allow yourself to remain static through fear of change, engage in efforts to stem overpopulation

**Area of Concern**
Foul transformations, tainted oceans, vile fecundity

</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Thuskchoon](/Deities.aspx?ID=586)
(Everglutton)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Thuskchoon is a towering slug with numerous limbs and ravenous maws that slithers through the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45), consuming all that he finds. In the wake of his near-mindless devastation, his mouths leaves swaths of disgusting tar. Sometimes, Everglutton stumbles across a portal to another plane, though these changes of scenery barely register with the qlippoth lord. He simply continues to indulge his insatiable hunger, uncaring of what (or who) he eats. 

Devotees of Thuskchoon expect little from their patron, but they crave the hidden knowledge sometimes revealed by his (and others’) wanton destruction. Scholars, pirates, and archaeologists are counted among his cultists, cloaking their endeavors under the guise of academia. They seek out ancient ruins to plunder, usually breaking timeworn architecture and antiquities in their heedless searching. Occasionally, an Everglutton cult gains enough power to direct Thuskchoon’s movements in small ways, sending the qlippoth lord to specific areas they believe conceal mighty secrets. Some worshippers are killed in the resulting demolition, but this never slows their fellow devotees’ pursuits, nor does it bother the indifferent Thuskchoon.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Qlippoth Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=30)

**Edicts** Devour everything at your disposal, discover hidden knowledge by any means, relish in your desires

**Anathema** Fast of your own accord, keep information to yourself, let food rot

**Area of Concern**
Blinding hunger, mindless consumption, revealed secrets

</column>

</column>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Yamasoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=587)
(The Polymorph Plague)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Yamasoth appears as a nightmarish octopus with a multitude of tentacles, each ending in a method of inflicting pain. His body is a single massive mouth filled with sharp teeth and stinging tongues. Eyes dot his form, and one huge red eye sits within his throat. Like all qlippoth lords, Yamasoth seeks to eradicate [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) by eliminating sin in mortals, but his methods involve corrupting powerful leaders until they doom their kingdoms and performing terrible fleshwarping experiments on hapless victims to release monsters upon civilization. Yamasoth has a grudging respect tempered with disdain for [Lamashtu](/Deities.aspx?ID=287) for her hand in creating monsters but hates almost all other deities. 

Cultists of the Polymorph Plague tend to be immoral inventors, doctors, and torturers. They push the boundaries of what mortal bodies can withstand, not fully realizing that Yamasoth grants them their abilities to cause a disruption in the ways that mortals experience and understand sin.

(The End of Innocence)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

When mortals were young, before [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278) conquered [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), Ardad Lili was already manipulating amorous and lustful mortals to swear fealty to her, amassing power from their souls. She fled the realm of [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=44) during the Exodus, taking up residence in Avernus and continuing to gather an army of damned souls and female devils who share her ambitions. The Serpent Muse has never forgotten the censures and cruel insults spewed by the other natives of Nirvana, and she seeks to someday rule not a layer of Hell, but a realm of the [heavens](/Planes.aspx?ID=41). Ardad Lili draws followers who are as passionate as herself.

<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Doloras](/Deities.aspx?ID=589)
(Our Lady of Pain)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Perhaps the cruelest of [Hell’s](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) divinities, the Sadistic Angel shares certain characteristics with the [velstracs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=111) who once resided in Hell— most notably, a drive to inflict physical pain upon living creatures. She does not inflict her torments out of anger or retribution; she practices her art with an unparalleled detachment and absence of emotion. Her followers are sadists, torturers who relish their grisly work, and those diabolists who summon velstracs rather than [devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=389). While she bears the title of Queen of the Night and commands power commensurate with the others, Our Lady in Pain is content with her station and holds no ambitions beyond ensuring she can continue her tortures uninterrupted.

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

[Hell’s](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) Valkyrie rules a domain spanning part of Dispater’s realm, commanding her legions of infernal soldiers as she sees fit. Eiseth operates outside Dispater’s rule, and she has forged powerful alliances both in Hell and beyond, having long ago rejected limitations placed upon her by others. Foremost among the Queens of the Night, she embodies battle, revenge, and wrath, and her ambitions are as lofty as her aerie of Widow’s Cry, where she forges souls of the damned into unequaled infernal legions answering to her alone.

<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Mahathallah](/Deities.aspx?ID=591)
(The Dowager of Illusions)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

While the other Queens of the Night are all fallen [celestials](/Traits.aspx?ID=551), Mahathallah was once among the most powerful of the [monitors](/Traits.aspx?ID=655): a [psychopomp](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458) usher in service of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290). Granted a glimpse of the end of her own existence, Mahathallah fled to the pits of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), finding some form of solace in Asmodeus’s counsel. Now the Dowager of Illusions, Mahathallah deals in death, fate, and vanity, pulling back the veils of lies that obscure the profound facts of existence. She teaches her followers to seek the truths underlying each falsehood, fomenting arrogance and callousness in them. She also serves as a distant, deliberate advisor to the other Queens of the Night.

epithet: The Skin That Walks on Teeth
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Ananshea](/Deities.aspx?ID=592)
(The Skin That Walks on Teeth)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Carnage and destruction are the domains of Ananshea. Their appetite for cruelty and violence can’t be met by the simplicity of battle, and so they must torment their foes before they can be even somewhat satisfied with victory. The Skin that Walks on Teeth is as impulsive and untamed as the animals they reign over, but takes their time once the initial skirmish is over to inflict the most pain possible upon a captured opponent. Screams of pain are akin to music to Ananshea; they despise long stretches of time without their favorite melody, so they are constantly on the hunt for prey.

Ananshea rules over a walled area of Xibalba filled with traps that tear limbs apart and pierce both body and mind, every intricate detail designed to torture the unfortunate souls who find themselves trapped in their territory. Their worshippers, violent and sadistic creatures devoted to bloodshed, roam the countryside in search of anyone they can cause pain in veneration of their god. This is most often done via torture, though violent deaths are also accepted. If there is a lack of possible victims, Ananshea’s cultists will turn on each other to appease their deity and their own sadistic desires.

The Skin That Walks on Teeth appears as a disembodied streak of red filled with dozens of gnashing animal jaws.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Annihilate any who would tame you, draw first blood, enjoy every kill to the fullest

**Anathema** Choose peaceful solutions over violence, hold back in a fight, tame a violent wild animal

**Area of Concern**
Brutality, torture, wild animals

anathema: Comfort the dying, seek immortality or unnatural longevity
edict: Accept the futility of life, destroy sentient undead, punish those who somehow avoid death
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Chamiaholom](/Deities.aspx?ID=593)
(Skull Staff)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

All mortals must die. This is the empirical truth championed by Chamiaholom, whose mission is to remind mortals that they will one day meet their end. Chamiaholom’s form is a towering [skeleton](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=472) over 14 feet tall, wielding a staff made from the skulls of his victims. He is sometimes glimpsed around sites of tragedy and destruction and stalks those who’ve survived near-death experiences, terrifying witnesses who mistake him for death incarnate. Chamiaholom’s followers wear dark paint on their faces to imitate the tormentor’s skeletal visage as they travel around Golarion preaching their message of despair. 

Chamiaholom despises sentient undead and commands their painful destruction. He has a particular animus toward liches. Worshippers of Chamiaholom can be cordial to followers of gods who disdain undeath and attack acolytes of undeath on sight. Killing someone who has attempted to defy death is not enough to satisfy Chamiaholom, who revels in the dread mortals feel when their time is come. His cultists capture and torture blasphemers, painstakingly using magic and detailed rituals to ensure any living quarry will not revive as an abomination. [Undead](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=3) are destroyed in a ritualistic manner that will send what is left of their souls directly to Chamiaholom to punish as he sees fit.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Accept the futility of life, destroy sentient undead, punish those who somehow avoid death

**Anathema** Comfort the dying, seek immortality or unnatural longevity

**Area of Concern**
Domination, hopelessness, mortality

</column>

anathema: Kill a monster that isn’t a direct threat, meddle in situations that don’t directly affect you
edict: Allow others to deal with their own mistakes, lead monsters to attack settlements, refrain from maintaining or fixing your possessions
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Charg](/Deities.aspx?ID=594)
(The Typhon Wheel)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

As it is the nature of mortals to destroy themselves, it is the nature of Charg to witness. The Typhon Wheel observes mortal catastrophes with keen interest and strict instruction to their followers to not interfere. Charg believes the decay of civilization and morals is a natural process and interference would be akin to the disruption of an unpolluted ecosystem.

Charg’s affinity for monsters is reflected in their chosen form, a swirling cluster of both monstrous and humanoid heads, limbs, and viscera. Their worshippers are charged to let such creatures run amok unless they directly attack Charg’s followers, wherein the faithful are permitted to intervene. Even in such cases, devotees of Charg must take care to do only the bare minimum to save themselves, lest they affect the lives of others and displease the tormentor.

Charg’s followers take care of only themselves and those they hold close, leaving the wider world to reap what it has sowed. Worshippers are sometimes gifted with visions of calamity as a test of their commitment to indifference. Quietly avoiding disaster without warning others is enough to pass the test but the most faithful accept their fates without protest.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Allow others to deal with their own mistakes, lead monsters to attack settlements, refrain from maintaining or fixing your possessions

**Anathema** Kill a monster that isn’t a direct threat, meddle in situations that don’t directly affect you

**Area of Concern**
Catastrophic decline, detrimental complacency, monsters

</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

anathema: Choose safety over trickery, forewarn the target of a prank
edict: Keep secrets from everyone, obtain what you desire, pursue your passions above all else
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Dachzerul](/Deities.aspx?ID=595)
(The Darkness Behind You)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Dachzerul believes in joy for everyone, especially himself. No pleasure should be denied, no joke interrupted, and no prank ruined by trivial matters such as safety or feelings. He is notable among the sahkil tormentors for his fickle moods, snapping from pleasant and exuberant to dark and obsessive within seconds, but can be instantly cheered by a deadly prank or a new object to obsess over. Towns unfortunate enough to draw his fleeting attention are hit by a deluge of wild phenomena such as raining [frogs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=494) or water turning into vomit that stops just as suddenly as it started. 

Dachzerul’s worshippers consist of individuals consumed by their obsessions, for they believe they should never be denied their desires. He is a patron of stalkers, whom he encourages into deeper fixation on their victims, and reckless pranksters. Dachzerul has also been known to aid serial killers, especially those with a flair for the dramatic. His domain over secrecy and trickery is the perfect combination to help his followers commit their crimes undetected, his power over luck and darkness aiding in hasty escapes. He is always ready to help anyone who asks as long as it’s in the name of his particular twisted brand of fun. 

When he deigns to be seen, Dachzerul appears as a slender man with vaguely simian features and a wide, off-putting grin. He sometimes wears a bright jester’s outfit of orange and white, though when he does, he is often clutching a blade or other dangerous weapon behind his back.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Keep secrets from everyone, obtain what you desire, pursue your passions above all else

**Anathema** Choose safety over trickery, forewarn the target of a prank

**Area of Concern**
Deadly pranks, stalkers, sudden death

</column>

anathema: Adopt new societal norms or innovations, save creatures from drowning
edict: Drown your enemies, refuse to change your mind in the light of new evidence, throw your body in the way of progress
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Hataam](/Deities.aspx?ID=596)
(The River Eater)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

The world flows too quickly, pushed into haste by innovation and technology. Civilizations rise and fall, bloodlines start and end, and even gods ascend and perish over time. The rapidity of life is distasteful to Hataam, who prefers the calmness of stagnant rivers and closed lakes. He often floats along murky waters as a mass of detritus and rotting limbs, leisurely watching for drownings and those who die of thirst. 

Hataam’s followers are similarly dissatisfied with the breakneck pace of the world, resentful of being left behind by progress and suppressed by unwanted change. They target those they blame for changing their lives for the worse, drowning their victims as a sacrifice to the River Eater. Those whose sins are deemed too great for the mercy of a quicker death are captured and left to die of thirst, though all bodies are disposed of in bodies of water. These polluted waters become a shrine to Hataam, bloated with corpses and overgrown algae.

His cultists refuse to change even after death, often becoming [ghosts](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=418) who repeat the patterns they followed in life. Those who do move on from the Universe sometimes refuse to follow psychopomps, even attempting to fight their guides to cling to what they once had.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Drown your enemies, refuse to change your mind in the light of new evidence, throw your body in the way of progress

**Anathema** Adopt new societal norms or innovations, save creatures from drowning

**Area of Concern**
Drought, drowning, stagnation

</column>

anathema: Fear the dark, grow too fond of your possessions and relationships
edict: Divest yourself of possessions and relationships whenever possible, foment despair and sorrow, rest among the dead
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Iggeret](/Deities.aspx?ID=597)
(She Who Was Lost)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Curiously, Iggeret has claimed that she was once a mortal girl who faked her own kidnapping to see how her family would react. After her parents killed themselves out of despair, She Who Was Lost entombed herself with their bodies, eventually joining them in death. None can confirm or deny this story. As a sahkil tormentor, Iggeret holds sway over dark and empty places such as pits and graves. She considers all who dwell in darkness to be her new family, and that they should join her in death. She Who Was Lost appears as a young girl with a gaunt, skeletal face and long, black hair. She weeps constant tears that stain her cheeks and simple white dress. 

Iggeret’s cultists call each other siblings. There is little structure to this so-called family except that Iggeret is the head, standing as a kind of big sister figure, despite her youthful form. Devotees of Iggeret often torment their non-cult loved ones with cruel trials to punish them for perceived slights or out of boredom. This can sometimes lead to indoctrination into the faith, while the rest are cast aside. Worshippers of She Who Was Lost try to practice austerity, retaining few possessions and dwelling in empty structures. Some even go so far as to sleep in open graves.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Divest yourself of possessions and relationships whenever possible, foment despair and sorrow, rest among the dead

**Anathema** Fear the dark, grow too fond of your possessions and relationships

**Area of Concern**
Darkness, empty places, pits

</column>

anathema: Hold a position of authority for more than a day, seek power for any reason but destroying those in power
edict: Crush the confident and arrogant, incite rebellion against just and unjust rulers alike, sow doubt from the shadows
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Nameless](/Deities.aspx?ID=598)
(Upon an Empty Throne)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Nameless is a whisper in the mind, a shadow in the corner of one’s eye, or a sudden noise with no source. They despise all creatures that dare claim sovereignty over others and work to bring down all who rule. Both righteous and the wicked figures of authority are targets for Nameless and their followers. One might point out the hypocrisy of Upon an Empty Throne’s own rise to power, but the tormentor’s wrath is reserved for those who actively seek positions of authority, and they claim their divinity came unbidden to them.

As a god of doubt and torment, Nameless murmurs fear into the ears of rulers, torturing them with insecurity and paranoia. If they find a target particularly vexing, they seed dreams of rebellion and power into the hearts of those who might rise up and destroy that ruler, though this usually creates yet another whose reign Nameless must end. Due to their great burden of stamping out all forms of rule, Upon an Empty Throne welcomes any who seek aid in bringing down local leaders no matter the ethics or morality. Nameless has been behind events ranging from the fall of great civilizations to sudden turns at small-town elections. No amount of governance is too small to escape their displeasure.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Crush the confident and arrogant, incite rebellion against just and unjust rulers alike, sow doubt from the shadows

**Anathema** Hold a position of authority for more than a day, seek power for any reason but destroying those in power

**Area of Concern**
Delusions of authority, doubt, torment

</column>

edict: Be amicable until the time is right to strike, give false hope to all, make the choice that will cause more pain
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Ozranvial](/Deities.aspx?ID=599)
(Despair’s Smile)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Ozranvial is one’s dearest friend until she isn’t. The sahkil tormentor of despair and sadism isn’t satiated by physical pain. Instead, she takes great pleasure in inserting herself into someone’s life and pulling it apart until they are utterly broken by her machinations. The intimacy of a relationship makes its destruction all the more beautiful. Ozranvial will foster a relationship over years and even decades to enjoy the devastating outcome of her cruelty. She rules over the restless spirits created from the emotional damage she inflicts, tormenting them even after death.

Followers of Ozranvial are sadists whose methods are mental rather than physical. While some may enjoy killing, death is never the end goal. A true worshipper seeks to completely destroy their victims emotionally. Despair’s Smile favors toxic friends, manipulative spouses, and anyone who psychologically torments others. Even those who do not worship her may gain her blessings, emboldening them to commit cruelties they wouldn’t have dreamed of before she whispered them into their minds. 

Ozranvial is unique among the sahkil tormentors as the only one who occasionally seeks the others for company. None can be sure if she genuinely enjoys the companionship of other sadists or is simply scheming against them.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Be amicable until the time is right to strike, give false hope to all, make the choice that will cause more pain

**Anathema** Apologize sincerely for your actions, show emotional vulnerability

**Area of Concern**
Despair, restless spirits, sadism

</column>

edict: Fiercely protect your privacy, rely only on yourself, waste time in frivolous pursuits
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Shawnari](/Deities.aspx?ID=600)
(The One Out of Place)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

The One Out of Place is an apt title for Shawnari, often forgotten even by the other sahkil tormentors. Little is known about Shawnari’s past, though it has been suggested that she abused her time as a [psychopomp](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458) by trapping souls in secret places where she could watch them wither in solitude. Her interest in isolation continues in her divine form, reigning quietly over loneliness and lost time.

Shawnari’s worshippers are commonly hermits and misanthropes who reject community in favor of solitude. There is little need for rituals or prayers—their loneliness is veneration enough. Shawnari’s followers can also be found in great cities where so many feel utterly alone. Desperately lonely creatures reach out for comfort and only find more emptiness in the tormentor’s arms. She teaches them to bury themselves in work or hobbies and lose all sense of the world, finding some facsimile of peace in time spent ignoring others.

Though content alone, Shawnari keeps careful watch of her foes and quietly times deadly strikes against them. She guides her followers to do the same, subtly maneuvering the enemies of other sahkil tormentors against them through anonymous tips and careful words.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Fiercely protect your privacy, rely only on yourself, waste time in frivolous pursuits

**Anathema** Keep strict track of your time, reach out to others when you feel alone

**Area of Concern**
Isolation, loneliness, lost time

</column>

anathema: Be obvious with your lies, reveal the truth without benefit
edict: Be sweet with lies and cruel with truths, corrupt the good intentions of others, master the art of manipulation
markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Velgaas](/Deities.aspx?ID=601)
(Minds in the Dark)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 320

Many an idealist with great hopes to change the world has had their dreams realized by meeting a handsome stranger on the road. Velgaas, who might be mistaken for a [nagaji](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=54) with his scaled skin and forked tongue, takes great care to learn their altruistic desires, then carefully plants the seeds of destruction into their kind hearts. Charities become cesspools of corruption, laboratories convert into dens of torture, and peaceful protests spiral into mob violence. 

Velgaas is the most methodical of the sahkil tormentors, preying on good intentions to fulfill his own evil designs. He rules over the aspects of emotional manipulation, emptiness, and ignorance, carefully selecting each word and action to best ingratiate himself with an unsuspecting victim. A honeyed tongue is his weapon, a mask of sincerity his shield. Few of the unfortunate souls who cross his path would ever believe how deeply he’s manipulated them. 

Corrupt politicians and courtiers are among Velgaas’s followers, but he prizes most the ones who fall into his hands unknowingly. The well-meaning authorities he influences might crack down on crime by way of an oppressive city guard or falsify evidence to finally execute a dangerous criminal.

**Anathema** Be obvious with your lies, reveal the truth without benefit

**Area of Concern**
Emotional manipulation, emptiness, ignorance

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Vermilion Mother](/Deities.aspx?ID=602)
(The Endless Root)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries Web Supplement](/Sources.aspx?ID=237) pg. 21

A rare few of those who’ve indulged in psychoactive plants have been met with horrifying visions of an old woman made of gnarled vines and roots, her sneering mouth full of fang-like thorns and pollen. Those unfortunate enough to meet Vermilion Mother are stalked in their sleep, choked nightly by her dream of a world overrun by plants and fungi. This sahkil tormentor cares little for those of flesh and blood, only that their bodies make for excellent fertilizer.

The Endless Root is worshipped by creatures with a strong preference for nature over civilization, pointing to industry and urbanization as roots of crime and pollution. She doesn’t care for worship but accepts it as a way to promote growth and propagation, enlisting her followers to protect plant life of all kinds. She is one of the few sahkil tormentors to have followers who don’t live wicked lifestyles, some of them even believing themselves benevolent as they work to keep nature unspoiled. But more often than not, her followers are fanatically devoted to the protection of plants to the point of killing anyone they judge as harmful to their territory, using them as sacrifices to feed the undergrowth.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Indulge in substances derived from psychoactive plants, kill any who would pollute without mercy, protect endangered plants above all else

**Anathema** Alter a piece of unspoiled land, purposely destroy plants

**Area of Concern**
Fecundity, overgrown places, psychoactive plants

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Xiquiripat](/Deities.aspx?ID=603)
(The Flying Scab)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries Web Supplement](/Sources.aspx?ID=237) pg. 21

Trapped deep beneath the Black Pyramid of Xibalba lives an embodiment of destruction known as the Flying Scab. Few have seen Xiquiripat and lived to tell tales of their constantly shifting guise, which changes from waves of congealed blood to a knot of fanged lampreys to a heaving mass of matted hair. Though this god’s form is fluid, their goals are less so, as Xiquiripat has had only one unbending desire since the dawn of their existence: to instill fear into every living thing they meet before condemning them to oblivion.

Xiquiripat’s worshippers and clerics roam the halls of the Black Pyramid, heavily armed and eager to honor their god with violence. These followers are not exempt from their deity’s lust for annihilation, but few have had the privilege to receive death at the hands of the one they worship. Xiquiripat’s rare devotees in the Universe are those with a predilection for murder and inflicting death. Though these individuals can display a veneer of social graces and blend in with their community, any sign of blessings from Xiquiripat is a stark warning that they are indiscriminate killers incapable of remorse for the pain they wreak on others.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Never show mercy, pursue your enemies to their end or yours, teach all to fear you

**Anathema** Accept surrender from an enemy, show cowardice or weakness

**Area of Concern**
Evisceration, fatal falls, massacres

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Restricted">
[Zipacna](/Deities.aspx?ID=604)
(The Mountain Below)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries Web Supplement](/Sources.aspx?ID=237) pg. 21

The lazy but violent Zipacna is one the largest sahkil tormentors, resembling a massive caiman with four eyes and unnervingly human hands. He prefers to lounge in the lusher regions of Xibalba being pampered by the unfortunate souls he’s forcefully inducted into his service, doling out vicious punishments when his demands aren’t met. Even the other sahkil tormentors avoid testing his fury, as his penchant for destructive violence overwhelms everything in his path.

Zipacna is worshipped by those who abuse their strength and power to take what they want, ranging from selfish warriors to irresponsible nobles. These acolytes never use their abilities to fulfill their responsibilities, only to indulge their petty desires at the expense of others. However, the indolent Zipacna is surprisingly efficient when he needs to be as sloppy work only leads to more work, which is a travesty in his eyes. Despite this, one is unlikely to witness Zipacna do any sort of work outside of acts of violence as his beleaguered servants tend to his every whim. It is said the last time the great caiman did so much as lift a stone was before the days of Azlant.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sakhil Tormentors](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=31)

**Edicts** Do only what you must, eat your fill, overpower anyone who challenges you

**Anathema** Serve others, allow laziness to create more work for yourself, refuse an indulgence

**Area of Concern**
Avoiding responsibility, shifting earth, violence

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Alglenweis](/Deities.aspx?ID=605)
(Princess of the Rime)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 322

Called the Princess of the Rime by some, Alglenweis is the most popular Sarkorian deity in the Realm of the Mammoth Lords; though, since the opening of the Worldwound, her worship in Sarkoris has dwindled. Her ties to [demonic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) entities like her father have placed her in a position of mistrust among Sarkorians who fear the return of a malevolent entity like Deskari.

Originally a localized deity popularized by the witches of the Stagheart clan, Alglenweis encourages her devotees to create and protect works of art, especially those with cultural significance. Her followers cherish winter as a time to work on projects and spend hours with friends and relatives out of the cold.

Today, Sarkorians who still follow the Princess of Rime celebrate their return to their homelands by reclaiming the crafting traditions of their people. They also encourage incursions into portions of the Sarkoris Scar still infested with demonic forces and outfit expeditions to ruins of old settlements in hopes of recovering artistic treasures, even after many years under demonic occupation. Those who venerate Alglenweis hope that, by serving as an active force in the ongoing reclamation efforts, they can also help return Alglenweis to the prominence she once enjoyed. The most common depictions of the goddess feature her as a gigantic snowy owl whose claws clutch a skein of yarn and whose feathers are made of ice.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sarkorian Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=15)

**Anathema** Destroy an artistic creation without providing something in its place, refuse to act if called upon

**Area of Concern**
Crafting, resolve, winter

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Dolok Darkfur](/Deities.aspx?ID=606)
(Champion of the Farheavens)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 322

Perhaps the most well-known and active of the Sarkorian deities, Dolok Darkfur is immensely old, possessed of nearly unmatched physical prowess, and is an active participant in battles to reclaim Sarkoris from the [demonic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) remnants of the Worldwound.

Dolok and his people, the Farheavens, fled to Iobaria following the Worldwound’s opening, and Dolok kept the Farheavens safe for many generations until his last god caller died during a period where Dolok had slipped into a depressive hibernation. The loss of his last true mortal contact only deepened Dolok’s depression, and he may have been lost to history forever had a group of archaeologists from the Pathfinder Society not sought out the Farheavens as part of a research project seeking out displaced Sarkorians.

Upon learning of Dolok’s existence, the Pathfinders reunited him with the Farheavens leader, Nelket, a young woman with the ability to become a god caller.

Since Dolok and Nelket’s bonding, the Farheavens clan has adopted a goblin tribe known as the Dragondwellers into their ranks. The combined might of Dolok, the Farheavens, and the Dragondwellers has made them one of the premier forces of reclamation in the Sarkoris Scar, both in combating demons directly and in sharing Dolok’s ancient knowledge to restore Sarkorian society

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Kagia](/Deities.aspx?ID=607)
(He Gathers In)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 322

Kagia takes the form of a hulking [bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=370) with two vulture heads. Though not a true deity, he is likely the closest among living spirit guides to claiming that ascendance. His half-brother, [Dolok Darkfur](/Deities.aspx?ID=606), is also a contender, but Dolok’s long period of inactivity has likely left the feathered bear with a smaller number of mortal followers and less interest in growing his spiritual power. As the only child ever born to Bear and Vulture (whose relationship lasted only a few mortal generations when clans they protected regularly intermarried), Kagia possesses immense physical power. Though he is easily roused to anger, the burly deity is most concerned with the collection of resources and their efficient use. Berry picking is sacred to him, and artisans work his image into the bottom of finely woven baskets in hopes of a bountiful harvest. His worshippers value self-sufficiency, and avoid wearying Kagia with petty requests, lest he drop into a torpor and leave them undefended. Priests of Kagia handle the interning of the dead, and they gather and redistribute the belongings of the deceased in a way that benefits the clan as a whole.

**Anathema** Kill for pleasure or glory, waste resources

**Area of Concern**
Efficiency, harvests, resourcefulness

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Ristrentho](/Deities.aspx?ID=608)
(Whose Blood Churns)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 322

Ristrentho, who appears as an otter with glittering scales, is popular with Sarkorian clans who fled down the Sellen River after the fall of Sarkoris. Today, their followers are eager to return to the banks of the Sarkora and West Sarkora Rivers. Ristrentho’s worshippers are content to settle along watercourses, though most prefer to live a transient life on houseboats and water caravans. Though not always the most altruistic of people, followers of Ristrentho hold no truck with fiends and quickly send obvious villains on their way. Devotees of Ristrentho tend towards optimism and playfulness but also selfmotivation, carving their own course through the world like a wild river.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sarkorian Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=15)

**Edicts** Clear obstacles to progress, spend part of each day in motion

**Anathema** Dam a river or stream, hold a creature against its will

**Area of Concern**
Forward progress, removing barriers, rivers

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Stag Mother of the Forest of Stones](/Deities.aspx?ID=609)
(She Who Listens)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 322

The Stag Mother of the Forest of Stones is quite possibly the oldest surviving spirit guide left in existence, certainly the oldest still actively worshipped in Sarkoris today. Long ago, she was the premier spirit worshipped in the region of Sarkoris known as the Stonewilds, and the druids of the Circle of Hierophants (or their distant predecessors) erected stone menhirs as monuments depicting the Stag Mother and her fellow spirit guides guiding and protecting the people of the region. When the Worldwound opened and demons poured forth, the Stag Mother gathered many Sarkorians to safety in her demesne, a region of the Stonewilds known as the Forest of Stones. Between the Stag Mother’s godly power and the might of the lesser spirit guides that gathered under her banner, the Forest of Stones may have stood as a bastion of nature capable of withstanding even the unprecedented demonic incursion, but sadly a group of druids in the region lost faith in their long-time protectors. When the horrific perversions of druidic faith known as the Stonewild Sect transformed themselves into undead, their twisted power spread through the bonds some shared with the spirit guides, killing many of the weaker guides instantly and transforming others into dark mockeries of their former selves. In despair, the Stag Mother retreated far from the front lines of the battle, sheltering a precious few. While her worship has once again begun to spread beyond her forest home, the Stag Mother’s trust of her human worshippers has been deeply undermined, and she demands that the spirit guides who live within her dominion do not form bonds with mortals or accept new god callers without her express permission.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Sarkorian Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=15)

**Edicts** Act as a responsible steward of your environment, provide shelter and succor for women and children in need

**Anathema** Harm a pregnant mother or innocent child, including animals

**Area of Concern**
Druids, motherhood, sacred nature

**Pantheons**
[The Spirit Wall](/Deities.aspx?ID=363)

</column>

<title level="1" right="Deity " pfs="Standard">
[Arundhat](/Deities.aspx?ID=610)
(The Sacred Perfume)
</title>

<row gap="medium">
<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Divine Mysteries](/Sources.aspx?ID=234) pg. 322

Arundhat is the god of blossoms and scent, most commonly worshipped in Vudra. While followers who worship deities of war or strength often consider Arundhat's portfolio a little lackluster, to those with the right knowledge, the humble flower can accomplish far more than a simple show of force. Arundhat's mastery of botanicals and scented oils can bring someone back from near death, sway a person to passion, or calm an agitated mob.

<column gap="tiny">

**Category** [Vudrani Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=17)

**Edicts** Offer appropriate flowers to other divinities, practice herbalism, tend to sacred flowers

**Anathema** Dispose of waste near flowers, dispose of withered flowers improperly, harvest flowers without offering the proper prayers

**Area of Concern**
Blossoms, diplomacy, scent

</column>
